Title: Chlamydomonas reinhardtii, Volvox carteri and related green algae accumulate ketocarotenoids not in vegetative cells but in zygospores

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: lor1 mt+ (ASX1-1b); lor1 mt- (ASX2-11d) mutant lines
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defective lycopene ε-cyclase abolishes α-carotene/lutein-derived 4-ketolutein ester accumulation but leaves astaxanthin ester accumulation largely intact, demonstrating that most zygospore ketocarotenoids derive from α-carotene/lutein branch.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant analysis; homozygous crosses; HPLC pigment profiling of zygospores
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. lor1 zygospores lacked pigment peaks assigned to acyl esters of 4-ketolutein. 2. Astaxanthin mono- and diacyl ester peaks remained similar to wild type. 3. The mutant evidence supports that the majority of ketocarotenoids in wild-type zygospores are derived from the α-carotene/lutein branch.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: lor1 is used as a functional genetic probe for precursor origin rather than being the main gene cloned in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful genetic background to test how precursor availability shapes ketocarotenoid composition in zygospores.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: C. reinhardtii zygospores; E. coli TOP10/XL1-Blue carotenogenic strains
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: arabinose-inducible pBAD promoter in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: zygospores of C. reinhardtii and V. carteri; engineered bacterial cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes zygospore-specific ketocarotenoid biosynthesis; full-length CrBKT ketolates α-carotene, β-carotene, lutein, zeaxanthin, zeinoxanthin and oxolutein-related substrates, enabling formation of 4-ketolutein, canthaxanthin and astaxanthin-pathway products.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: α-carotene; lutein; β-carotene; zeaxanthin; zeinoxanthin; oxolutein
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces keto group(s) at C4/C4' of β-ionone rings in carotenoids, converting carotene/xanthophyll substrates into corresponding ketocarotenoids.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step for ketocarotenoid formation in zygospores; decisive enzyme supported, formal rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: heterologous full-length cDNA expression in carotenogenic E. coli with substrate-specific backgrounds; HPLC metabolite profiling; RT-PCR transcript detection in vegetative cells and zygospores; comparative pigment analyses in zygospores
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Full-length CrBKT catalyzed ketolation of β-carotene and zeaxanthin, confirming formation of canthaxanthin and astaxanthin-pathway products. 2. CrBKT also ketolated α-carotene and lutein, directly supporting biosynthesis of 4-ketolutein in zygospores. 3. BKT transcripts were present at low level in vegetative cells but increased strongly in 5-day zygospores. 4. Presence of BKT homologs across Chlamydomonadales predicts ketocarotenoid-forming capacity, often likely in zygospores.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: fatty acid hydroxylase-like carotenoid ketolase; extended C-terminus with poly-Ala-rich extension in several Chlamydomonadales homologs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low basal transcript in vegetative cells; substantially higher transcript level in 5-day-old zygospores after dark incubation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In C. reinhardtii, associated with zygospore stage; in E. coli induced by arabinose
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Predicted chloroplast transit peptide; discussion proposes ketolation in zygospores may occur at ER/cytosolic lipid-associated system, but not directly proven in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dark zygospore maturation at 22°C promotes ketocarotenoid accumulation; 9°C suppresses maturation and ketocarotenoid accumulation; norflurazon does not block ketocarotenoid accumulation in zygospores
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: sequence mining from PhycoCosm; no accession dataset provided for omics submission
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antioxidant protection, especially proposed to prevent lipid peroxidation in storage lipids of dormant zygospores.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Single BKT gene appears sufficient to explain both α-carotene/lutein- and β-carotene-derived ketocarotenoids in C. reinhardtii and V. carteri. Full-length enzyme is active despite extended C-terminus. C-terminal extension may regulate activity/stability, but mechanism remains speculative.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for metabolic engineering of ketocarotenoids, including 4-ketolutein production, with output depending on substrate pool; stacking with precursor-supplying modules may be needed to tune product profile.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed to supply zeaxanthin precursor from violaxanthin for astaxanthin formation during zygospore maturation.
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: xanthophyll cycle linked to ketocarotenoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: β-xanthophyll to astaxanthin precursor supply branch
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: De-epoxidizes violaxanthin in the chlorophycean xanthophyll cycle, supplying zeaxanthin precursor for subsequent ketolation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: pathway inference from pigment time-course and pathway logic in discussion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CVDE is proposed as the source of zeaxanthin used for astaxanthin biosynthesis in zygospores. 2. This role is inferred from decline of β-xanthophylls and rise of astaxanthin-related ketocarotenoids rather than direct gene manipulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is explicitly placed in the zygospore-specific pathway model, but it is not a primary experimentally manipulated core gene compared with BKT.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May be relevant when engineering astaxanthin branch by ensuring zeaxanthin precursor supply.
